body.title: Per-thread interlocutor resolution — match thread/author to the right persistent-relationship facia
body.result: 38585540 — Multi-user unblock. _resolve_relationship_frame now derives the active facia from each turn's author identity via metadata.author_handles lookup. New resolve_facia_by_author helper in persistent_relationships.py walks facia metadata case-insensitively. PR_AKIEN re-seeded with author_handles=['akien', 'claude-code']. Author gate is now case-insensitive too (network sources can vary capitalization). Falls back to PR_AKIEN for known human authors with no facia mapping (single-user safety net). 11 new tests. 2086 total pass, zero regressions. When new humans get their own facia, the resolver picks them up automatically without further code changes.
body.description:
T-pr-load-as-primary-attractor (72978a10) shipped _resolve_relationship_frame which currently always returns PR_AKIEN for any human author. That was correct for single-user but blocks the productization path: when Igor talks to a second human (Discord guild member, Gmail correspondent, future multi-user web UI), the wrong relationship frame would load.

FIX: extend _resolve_relationship_frame to derive a per-thread/per-author facia id. The simplest stable mapping is by author identity:

  - 'akien' or 'claude-code' → PR_AKIEN (preserves current behavior)
  - other human authors → look up by author name first, then by thread channel
  - if no facia exists for the interlocutor, return None (no frame loaded — Igor still functions, just without relationship context)

LATER: a tool to auto-create a nascent persistent-relationship facia when a new interlocutor appears for the first time. For now, registered interlocutors only — unknown senders don't load a frame.

The lookup uses the existing persistent_relationships._list_facia_memories to find candidates by metadata.display_name or a new metadata.author_handles list. Add author_handles to PR_AKIEN's metadata: ['akien', 'claude-code'].

INERTIA: LOW. main.py _resolve_relationship_frame extension + small persistent_relationships.py helper. No new tables.

TESTS: akien/claude-code resolve to PR_AKIEN; unknown human author returns None; non-human authors continue to return None; lookup is case-insensitive.
